name: Sam Smith
description:
'''Samuel Frederick Smith''' (born 19 May 1992) is an English singer and songwriter. In 2012, they{{Efn|Smith is [[non-binary]] and uses ''[[they/them]]'' pronouns.<ref name="pronouns" /><ref name="pronouns 2" />}} rose to prominence when they featured on [[Disclosure (band)|Disclosure]]'s breakthrough single "[[Latch (song)|Latch]]", which peaked at number eleven on the [[UK Singles Chart]]. The following year, they featured on [[Naughty Boy]]'s single "[[La La La (Naughty Boy song)|La La La]]", which became a number one single in the UK.

Smith's debut studio album, ''[[In the Lonely Hour]]'' (2014), was released through [[Capitol Records UK]] and debuted at number one on the [[UK Albums Chart]]. The album's lead single, "[[Lay Me Down (Sam Smith song)|Lay Me Down]]", was released prior to "La La La". The album's second single, "[[Money on My Mind]]", became their second number one single in the UK. Its third single, "[[Stay with Me (Sam Smith song)|Stay with Me]]", was internationally successful, reaching number one in the UK and number two on the US [[Billboard Hot 100|''Billboard'' Hot 100]], while subsequent singles "[[I'm Not the Only One]]" and "[[Like I Can]]" reached the top ten in the UK. The album won four awards at the [[57th Annual Grammy Awards]], including [[Best Pop Vocal Album]], [[Best New Artist]], [[Record of the Year]], [[Grammy Award for Song of the Year|Song of the Year]], and nominations for [[Grammy Award for Album of the Year|Album of the Year]] and [[Best Pop Solo Performance]].

Smith's song "[[Writing's on the Wall (Sam Smith song)|Writing's on the Wall]]" served as the [[theme music|theme]] for the [[James Bond in film|James Bond]] film ''[[Spectre (2015 film)|Spectre]]'' (2015), and won Smith a [[Golden Globe Award for Best Original Song|Golden Globe Award]] and an [[Academy Award for Best Original Song]]. Smith's second studio album, ''[[The Thrill of It All (Sam Smith album)|The Thrill of It All]]'' (2017), debuted atop the UK and US album charts. The album's lead single, "[[Too Good at Goodbyes]]", reached number one in the UK and Australia and number four in the US. Following the 2018 single "[[Promises (Calvin Harris and Sam Smith song)|Promises]]" (with [[Calvin Harris]]), which peaked at number one in the UK, Smith released "[[Dancing with a Stranger]]" (with [[Normani]]) in 2019, which peaked within the top ten in the UK and the US, also receiving a nomination for [[Brit Award for Song of the Year|Song of the Year]] at the [[2020 Brit Awards]]. The singles, along with "[[How Do You Sleep? (Sam Smith song)|How Do You Sleep?]]", would precede the release of their third studio album, ''[[Love Goes]]'' (2020). In 2022, Smith's single "[[Unholy (Sam Smith and Kim Petras song)|Unholy]]" (with [[Kim Petras]]), would become their first number one single in the US and won the [[Grammy Award for Best Pop Duo/Group Performance]]. The song would precede their fourth album, ''[[Gloria (Sam Smith album)|Gloria]]'' (2023).

Smith's [[List of awards and nominations received by Sam Smith|numerous accolades]] include five [[Grammy Award]]s, three [[Brit Awards]], three [[Billboard Music Award|''Billboard'' Music Awards]], and an [[American Music Award]], as well as a [[Golden Globe]] and an [[Academy Award]]. On the UK Albums Chart, ''In the Lonely Hour'' was the [[List of best-selling albums of the 2010s in the United Kingdom#Best-selling debut albums|best-selling debut album of the 2010s]] and the [[List of best-selling albums of the 2010s in the United Kingdom|sixth best-selling album of the decade]], while collectively Smith's albums spent the [[List of UK Albums Chart number ones of the 2010s#Artists with the most weeks at number one|fourth-most weeks at number one in the 2010s]], behind [[Ed Sheeran]], [[Adele]] and [[Eminem]]. Smith is the first openly [[non-binary]] musician to both release a song that reached No. 1 on the ''Billboard'' Hot 100 and to win a Grammy Award.
